person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
geni.com Henry I of France, King of France French: Henri I de France, roi de France, Latin: Henricus I Franciae, rex Franciae, Spanish: Dn. Enrique I Capeto, King of France Also Known As: "Henri Capet", "Roi de France", "Prince of France" 
Birthdate: May 04, 1008 Birthplace: Muelan, Paris, Orleannais, West Francia (now Ile-de-France, France) 
Death: August 04, 1060 (52) Palais de Vitry-aux-Loges, Vitry-aux-Loges, Centre, France Place of Burial: Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, France Immediate Family: 
Son of Robert II Capet, "the Pious" king of the Franks and Constance d'Arles, queen consort of the Franks 
Husband of Matilda of Frisia, queen consort of the Franks and Anna of Kiev, Queen Consort of the Franks 
Fiancé of Mathilde de Germanie 

Father of
 N.N.; 
Emma Capet de France; 
Robert Capet de France; 
Hugues I 'Magnus', Comte de Vermandois and 
Philip I, king of France 

Brother of Emergarde de Auvergne; Hedwige de France, comtesse d'Auxerre; Hugues, roi associé de France; Adela of France, countess of Flanders; Constance de France, heritiere de Dammartin; Robert I le Vieux, duc de Bourgogne and Eudes de France « less Half brother of .... Capet Occupation: King of France (1031-1060), Roi de France (1031-1060), King of France, King of the Franks, King f France, Kung av Frankrike 1031 - 1060, King, Roi des Francs, duc de Bourgogne, Koning van Frankrijk (1031-1060)
LESS


Henry I (4 May 1008-4 August 1060) was King of the Franks from 1031 to his death. The royal demesne of France reached its smallest size during his reign, and for this reason he is often seen as emblematic of the weakness of the early Capetians. This is not entirely agreed upon, however, as other historians regard him as a strong but realistic king, who was forced to conduct a policy mindful of the limitations of the French monarchy.

«b»Reign«/b»
A member of the House of Capet, Henry was born in Reims, the son of King Robert II (972-1031) and Constance of Arles (986-1034). He was crowned King of France at the Cathedral of Reims on 14 May 1027, in the Capetian tradition, while his father still lived. He had little influence and power until he became sole ruler on his father's death.

The reign of Henry I, like those of his predecessors, was marked by territorial struggles. Initially, he joined his brother Robert, with the support of their mother, in a revolt against his father (1025). His mother, however, supported Robert as heir to the old king, on whose death Henry was left to deal with his rebel sibling. In 1032, he placated his brother by giving him the duchy of Burgundy which his father had given him in 1016.

In an early strategic move, Henry came to the rescue of his very young nephew-in-law, the newly appointed Duke William of Normandy (who would go on to become William the Conqueror), to suppress a revolt by William's vassals. In 1047, Henry secured the dukedom for William in their decisive victory over the vassals at the Battle of Val-ès-Dunes near Caen; however, Henry would later support the barons against William until the former's death in 1060.

In 1051, William married Matilda, the daughter of the count of Flanders, which Henry saw as a threat to his throne. In 1054, and again in 1057, Henry invaded Normandy, but on both occasions he was defeated.

Henry had three meetings with Henry III, Holy Roman Emperor-all at Ivois. In early 1043, he met him to discuss the marriage of the emperor with Agnes of Poitou, the daughter of Henry's vassal. In October 1048, the two Henries met again and signed a treaty of friendship. The final meeting took place in May 1056 and concerned disputes over Theobald III and County of Blois. The debate over the duchy became so heated that Henry accused the emperor of breach of contract and subsequently left. In 1058, Henry was selling bishoprics and abbacies, ignoring the accusations of simony and tyranny by the Papal legate Cardinal Humbert. Despite his efforts, Henry I's twenty-nine-year reign saw feudal power in France reach its pinnacle.

King Henry I died on 4 August 1060 in Vitry-en-Brie, France, and was interred in Basilica of St Denis. He was succeeded by his son, Philip I of France, who was 7 at the time of his death; for six years Henry's queen Anne of Kiev ruled as regent. At the time of his death, he was besieging Thimert, which had been occupied by the Normans since 1058.

«b»Marriages«/b»
Henry I was betrothed to Matilda, the daughter of Conrad II, Holy Roman Emperor, but she died prematurely in 1034. Henry then married Matilda of Frisia, but she died in 1044, following a Caesarean section. Casting further afield in search of a third wife, Henry married Anne of Kiev on 19 May 1051. They had four children:

1.) Philip I (23 May 1052-30 July 1108).

2.) Emma (1054-1109).

3.) Robert (c. 1055-1060).

4.) Hugh "the Great" of Vermandois (1057-1102).


https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henry_I_of_France

zh.lifeSummary: 當Henri Ier roi des Francs於1008年5月4日出生在法國哈帕格-亞登芒內萊姆斯時，他的父親Robert II le Pieux roi de France36歲，母親Constance d'Arles36歲。他於1034年與Mathilde von Friesland在法國結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1060年8月4日在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瑟-賽吉登尼斯享年52歲，埋葬在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瑟-賽吉登尼斯。

nameMeanings.name: Henri
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Norwegian vernacular form of   Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Henri Ier roi des Francs was born on 4 May 1008, in Reims, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, France, his father, Robert II le Pieux roi de France, was 36 and his mother, Constance d'Arles, was 36. He married Mathilde von Friesland in 1034, in France. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 4 August 1060, in Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, France, at the age of 52, and was buried in Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henri Ier roi des Francs est né le 4 mai 1008, à Reims, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, France, son père, Robert II le Pieux roi de France, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Constance d'Arles, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Mathilde von Friesland en 1034, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 4 août 1060, à Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, France, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Henri Ier roi des Francs am 4. Mai 1008 in Reims, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert II le Pieux roi de France 36 und seine Mutter Constance d'Arles 36. Er hat 1034 in Frankreich Mathilde von Friesland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 4. August 1060 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Frankreich und wurde in Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Henri Ier roi des Francs è nato il il 4 maggio 1008 nel Reims, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, il suo padre, Robert II le Pieux roi de France, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Constance d'Arles, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Mathilde von Friesland nel 1034, nel Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 4 agosto 1060, nel Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Francia, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolto a Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Henri Ier roi des Francsが1008年5月4日にフランスチャンプアグネ-アルデンマルンレイムズで生まれた時，父Robert II le Pieux roi de Franceは36歳，母Constance d'Arlesは36歳であった。1034年にフランスでMathilde von Frieslandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1060年8月4日にフランスiレ-デ-フランスセアイネ-サイント-デニスで52歳で死去し，フランスiレ-デ-フランスセアイネ-サイント-デニスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Henri Ier roi des Francs(이)가 1008년 5월 4일 프랑스 챔파그니-아르데니 마니 레임스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert II le Pieux roi de France(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Constance d'Arles(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1034년 프랑스에서 Mathilde von Friesland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1060년 8월 4일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 세이인-세인트-데니스에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 세이인-세인트-데니스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henri Ier roi des Francs nasceu em 4 de maio de 1008, em Reims, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, França, seu pai, Robert II le Pieux roi de France, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Constance d'Arles, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Mathilde von Friesland em 1034, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 4 de agosto de 1060, em Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, França, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henri Ier roi des Francs родился 4 мая 1008 г. в местности римс, марн, чампагне-арденн, Франция, его отцу по имени Robert II le Pieux roi de France было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Constance d'Arles, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Mathilde von Friesland в 1034 г. в местности Франция. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 4 августа 1060 г. в местности сине-сеинт-денис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности сине-сеинт-денис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция.

Se casó con Mathilde von Friesland en 1034, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 4 de agosto de 1060, en Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Francia.
